title: Bound orchestrator output to prevent session-killing token limit errors
source_context: /insights patterns section identified output-size blocking as killing 3+ sessions unrecoverably. Our visual.py renderers and orchestrate.py sprint output are likely culprits. [PRIORITY: /insights report identified as top-ROI fix]
description: From /insights: multiple sessions were entirely blocked by 'API output token max exceeded' errors. Root cause: Rick's CTO orchestrator skill renders large status displays (sprint dashboards, team boards, evolution reports) inline. Fix: for any output > 200 lines or > 50k chars, write to a file under .cto/reports/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM-<kind>.md and print only a 10-line summary to chat with a link to the file. Applies to: sprint dashboard, prometheus status, team board, persona check, visual team/sprint renderers.
proposed_changes: Add a render_bounded() helper in visual.py: if output > threshold, write to .cto/reports/ and return a short summary + file path. Refactor render_team_board, render_sprint, and prometheus status output to route through render_bounded when content exceeds 200 lines.
